Video.summary: A couple want to evict their violent 31-year-old son, while their son blames his parents for his financial struggles.
Video.title: Our Son is a Violent Mooch

Image.alt: Our Son is a Violent Mooch
Our Son is a Violent Mooch
Our Son is a Violent Mooch
Our Son is a Violent Mooch
Our Son is a Violent Mooch
Our Son is a Violent Mooch
Our Son is a Violent Mooch

Video.summary: Joni claims that Gina used to be one of her besties and that they were inseparable, from spending birthday trips out of town to girls’ nights out, often ending in Gina crashing at Joni’s house. Joni says all that changed when 23- year-old Gina began dating Joni’s 49-year-old father, David, behind her back and giving birth to David’s child. Joni says she feels betrayed by Gina and her father, even going as far as sharing her dislike for her former friend and father’s newfound romance on TikTok. Gina says the picture Joni paints of them being best friends is all a lie, but in any event, Joni needs to accept her relationship with Joni’s father. David says despite his daughter’s disapproval, he is going to continue being in a committed relationship with Gina, but that doesn’t mean he can’t also have a relationship with his daughter.
Video.title: 49-Year-Old Dad Dating Friend

Video.summary: A racist man tries to change his ways. A man finds out he is biracial.
Video.title: Racism Follow Up

Video.summary: Imagine your pregnant teen daughter running away to be with the baby's father -- an older man whom she met online. For Heidi, this became reality when her 15-year-old daughter, Brittney, went missing for five weeks. Dr. Phil put private investigator and former FBI Agent Harold Copus on the case to find the missing teen. See the confrontation when Harold finally locates her and her 34-year-old boyfriend. Then, find out why Brittney was even more upset when she found out what was in store for her. It's been two months; has Brittney's relationship with her mother improved? Next, Kim, 15, has run away six times in the last year, most recently making it halfway across the country to be with her married boyfriend. Why didn't her parents look for her, and why does she say she'll do anything to get away from them? Can this family learn to get things back on track? Think your child is being targeted by a sexual predator? Don’t miss Dr. Phil’s warning signs!
Video.title: Runaway Dramas

Video.summary: Bridget says she’s not sure what to believe about the relationship between her 38-year-old brother-in-law, Kevin, and 16-year-old daughter, Grace.Dr. Phil says Bridget is a parent in denial and in need of a wake-up call. He sits down one-on-one with Kevin. How does Kevin explain his actions?
Video.title: Uncle Kevin Responds (Part 2)

Video.summary: If you think pretty people are immune to heartache, think again. The headlines are filled with stories of striking women who were devastated by infidelity, despite their outward beauty. Dr. Phil sits down with Joey, a husband and father, who says he can’t pass up a pretty face. His wife, Shannon, says he’s slept with close to 100 women since their wedding day. The personal trainer justifies his actions by saying Shannon is overweight and not hot enough for him. Will he get a wake-up call about his cheating ways? Or will his wife take him back yet again? See the shocking text messages to Joey’s mistress, and hear Dr. Phil’s strong message for the hapless husband when a disturbing secret is revealed. Can Dr. Phil encourage Joey to choose another path and look beyond the physical?
Video.title: Beauty Isn’t Enough

Video.summary: Moms are accused of bullying their own children.
Video.title: Bully Moms

Video.summary: Tiffany says her relationship with her son’s father, Erik, was destroyed by his constant lying and empty promises, to the point where she completely cut him off and has not seen him in over three years. Erik admits he has lied to his ex-girlfriends. Then, just this past year, Erik dated Sheka who says after doing research midway through their relationship, she uncovered his lies and put a stop to their wedding plans. So, when did Erik begin to con and deceive people? As Erik is accused of one lie more shocking than the next, his mom, Jody, says her son has been lying since he was a child. And, don't miss part 2, when more women come forward from Erik’s past.
Video.title: Our Lying Ex-Boyfriend

Video.summary: You've heard people complain, "If I only knew then what I know now, I wouldn't be in this mess," but what about those times when you choose not to see the writing on the wall? Dr. Phil meets couples who say their relationships went from bad to worse after they said, "I do," and now their families are broken.
Video.title: How to Fix a Broken Family

Image.alt: How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family
How to Fix a Broken Family

Video.summary: Can Dr. Phil convince a 24 yo NYU grad that he’s not a cyborg from the future?
Video.title: My Son Believes He’s a Cyborg

Image.alt: My Son Believes He’s a Cyborg
My Son Believes He’s a Cyborg
My Son Believes He’s a Cyborg
My Son Believes He’s a Cyborg
My Son Believes He’s a Cyborg
My Son Believes He’s a Cyborg
My Son Believes He’s a Cyborg

Video.summary: TV Star Nicholas Brendon has been starring in his own private alcohol-fueled drama which has led to multiple arrests and been documented in celebrity magazines, social media and blogs. Nicholas sits down with Dr. Phil -- and abruptly walks offstage. Is Nicholas ready to take the steps needed to save his life and career?
Video.title: TV Star's Alcoholism Spirals

Video.summary: From marital spats to parenting disagreements, learn the biggest mistakes you're making.
Video.title: Top 5 Mistakes You’re Making

Video.summary: From spending $600,000 on 3-year-old beauty queens, to pushy stage sisters and an ambitious boxing coach — today's guests say they go to the extremes to help the youngest members of their family excel. Meet two fathers, one whose full-time job is managing his tiny stars' careers, and the other who hand makes each and every one of his 4-year-old daughter's costumes. Are these dads bonding with their daughters or simply pushing too hard? Plus, meet several women who defiantly criticize child beauty pageants. And, hear from one overzealous sister and the father of a boxing champion once ranked first in the world. Can Dr. Phil convince these guests to step aside and strive for balance in their children's lives?
Video.title: Competing For The Title

Video.summary: Dr. Phil tackles two news stories that have captivated millions. First up, is it ever appropriate to confront your child’s bullies? That’s what James Jones says he did when he boarded his 12-year-old daughter, Chatari’s, school bus, and the video of his tirade went viral! James says some of the students terrorized his child, who suffers from cerebral palsy, and his actions resulted in two second-degree misdemeanor charges against him. CNN contributor Roland Martin joins the show, and he says James should be named Father of the Year for protecting Chatari, who was reportedly hit, called names and had condoms thrown at her. Roland goes head-to-head with attorney Areva Martin, who says James’s behavior was unwarranted. Did this dad cross the line? What does Dr. Phil think? Plus, the disappearance of David Hartley reads like a murder mystery. His wife, Tiffany, says they were on vacation when David was shot in the head by Mexican pirates while jet skiing. His body was never recovered, and Mexican authorities question if the event really occurred. Some people wonder if Tiffany was involved, but she maintains her innocence. Find out why she declined a lie detector test, and hear Dr. Phil’s strong message for her.
Video.title: Dad's Bus Confrontation

Video.summary: Amanda Knox spent four years locked in an Italian prison for the murder of her British roommate Meredith Kercher. Dubbed “Foxy Knoxy” by the press, she became one of the most famous cases of wrongful conviction. She tells her story, as she fights to save the life of Texas death row inmate Rodney Reed. This program contains graphic discussion about a criminal case. Viewer discretion advised.
Video.title: Amanda Knox: Freeing The Innocent

Video.summary: He’s a tough-guy afraid of loosing his freedom. She’s been through two ugly divorces. So when the “M” word comes up, they head for the hills. Join these commitment-challenged singles as Dr. Phil uncovers the reasons why they won’t take the plunge. You’ll also meet a man who, after a four years in a loving relationship, doesn’t see the need to get married. To him it’s just a piece of paper. To her it’s a lot more. Find out if it’s really a “deal breaker.”
Video.title: Commitment Phobia

Video.summary: A divorced couple secretly remarries, but did they make a mistake- again?
Video.title: Make It or Break It

Video.summary: Meddling moms who track and monitor their children's lives.
Video.title: Meddling Moms

Video.summary: Many women are itching to run to the altar, but Dr. Phil’s guests say they’re ready to run for the hills! Kelly is a self-proclaimed runaway bride. She’s been engaged four times but says she gets cold feet before she makes it down the aisle. Rick, her current fiancé, proposed four years ago and is still waiting for her to set a wedding date. Kelly believes that most marriages end in divorce, and she doesn’t want to become another statistic. Should Rick wait for her to make up her mind, or should he cut his losses and move on? Then, Chad and Candi have been engaged for over five years and have two daughters, but Chad says he’s not ready to tie the knot. He says weddings are too expensive, but Candi says he racked up $49,000 in loans for a car, a camper and a new deck for the house. Will Chad break down and give his fiancée the wedding of her dreams? Plus, Dr. Phil talks to a woman who says her fiancé left her six days before the wedding and stuck her with a $150,000 bill -- an allegation he totally denies.
Video.title: Runaway Bride, Runaway Groom

Video.summary: A woman attempts to confront her cheating husband with his alleged mistresses.
Video.title: Pablo’s Other Women

Video.summary: Is there something you’ve been hanging on to for just a little too long? Clothes you haven’t fit into since high school? Boxes of stuff you haven’t looked at in years? Dr. Phil’s guests say their loved ones need to call the junk yard and “Get rid of it!” Kim says her husband, Paul’s, Star Warshobby has to go. With over 6,000 pieces in his collection, Paul spends 30 hours a week playing with his action figures, building models and talking to friends about Star Wars. He even had a light saber sword fight at their wedding, so shouldn’t Kim have known what she was getting into? Next, Larry and Sheila married four months ago, but Sheila says she had no idea she was getting hitched to a hoarder. Larry says she’s got it all wrong -– she’s the pack rat! With some of their favorite collections rolled out on stage, can Dr. Phil help them negotiate what goes and what stays? Plus, meet Terri, who has four storage units and two garages full of sentimental memories -– like a rubber chicken and her father’s spine X-rays! She spends so much money storing her stuff, that she can’t even afford health insurance. What’s at the heart of her hoarding? Don’t miss this hilarious look at how collections can get out of control!
Video.title: Get Rid Of It!

Video.summary: George and Cindy Anthony speak out for the first time about their daughter, Casey's trial.
Video.title: Cindy and George Anthony:The Interview

Video.summary: Parents say their daughter's home is unsafe for grandson.
Video.title: Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!

Image.alt: Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!

Video.summary: Do slow drivers make you crazy? Do you start to lose it when cell phone talkers are too loud? People are more on edge than ever, with the slightest thing setting them off.
Video.title: Anger Management

Video.summary: "I wanted to make someone jealous." "I was bored." "I wanted to feel closer to God." These are just a few of the 237 reasons why women say they have sex. Author and clinical psychologist Dr. Cindy Meston and Dr. Jennifer Berman, a sexual health expert, join Dr. Phil to talk about women and sex, and offer advice to couples with differing libidos.
Video.title: The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex

Image.alt: The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex

Video.summary: These parents say their precious daughters have turned into bullies. Dr. Phil spells out how "commando parenting" will reshape the behavior of their "little mean girls."
Video.title: Little Mean Girls

Video.summary: Some believe self-censorship is a big problem today, particularly in light of the idea that someone can be "canceled" for their words -- blocked from having a prominent public platform or career for sharing their opinion. Dr. Phil interviews Nate, a former Idaho police officer, who claims he was fired after making a TikTok video about NBA superstar LeBron James that went viral and has been viewed over 4 million times. Nate explains how the fall-out from the video affected him and his family, too. Dr. Phil also speaks with conservative political commentator Tomi Lahren, who says she recently had to be protected by state police from student protestors at the University of New Mexico. Greg Lukianoff, president of the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression, says he believes Americans have lost the ability to discuss their differences rationally. And, Dr. Shaun Harper, founder and Executive Director of the USC Race and Equity Center, says he is firmly against hate speech and feels people need to be more mindful about the effects of their words.
Video.title: You Can’t Say That! Pt 1

Video.summary: Do you worry your teen daughter could throw away everything she's worked for - all for young love? Dina says she gave her daughter, Sammie, everything she'd ever wanted, but the 19-year-old is now only interested in one thing: her 20-year-old boyfriend, Chris, who has a criminal record. Despite cutting the teen off financially, Dina says her ultimatums fell flat and fears they may only have pushed Sammie further into the arms of a boyfriend she thinks is bad news and may be dangerous. Sammie says her controlling mother and sheltered upbringing are the real problems - but, can she explain the bruises on her body? Then, Samantha is a teen who was blinded by her love affair with her allegedly abusive boyfriend, Aaron. Don't miss her update and somber words for Sammie, whom she fears may be walking blindly down the perilous path she says she knows too well.
Video.title: Is My Teen Blinded By Love?

Image.alt: Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?

Video.summary: One man says he caught his wife cheating – on audiotape – but she denies the accusations. Who’s really telling the truth? Dr. Phil gets to the bottom of the alleged affair on tape. Can this relationship survive without trust?
Video.title: Affair, Lies, & Audiotape

Video.summary: In Part 3 of the Man Camp Series, couples fighting to save their marrages deal with family issues, infidelity, and unexpected visitors.
Video.title: Man Camp Part 3

Video.summary: In the summer of 2014, a former middle school English teacher was arrested on suspicion of statutory rape and contributing to the delinquency of a minor after police found her in a motel room with a 15-year-old former student. In a Dr. Phil exclusive, Jennifer Caswell speaks out for the first time. How does she explain her relationship with her former student?
Video.title: Teacher Scandal Confession

Video.summary: Rylee, 26, admits she’s a sugar baby and says she has no shame in dating wealthy, married men she meets online in exchange for money and lavish gifts -- and three years ago, she even convinced her mother, Tanya, to join her. Dr. Phil sits down with this mother/daughter sugar baby team, who insist they never have sex with their clients. What “services” do they say these men are paying for? And how does their family feel about their lifestyle? Then, Ashley admits she has had a side job as a sugar baby for years but has yet to tell her fiancé, Cesar -- and she wants Dr. Phil to help her confess. How will Cesar respond? And, reality TV star Kendra Wilkinson opens up about her husband, Hank Baskett’s, alleged affair with a transsexual. Does she believe the rumors? And, can her marriage survive the turmoil?
Video.title: The Secret World of Sugar Babies

Image.alt: The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies

Video.summary: An engagement symbolizes a commitment you make to one special person. But Dr. Phil's guests are accused of the ultimate betrayal: cheating on their spouse-to-be right before walking down the aisle.
Video.title: Cheating Fiancés
